frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: canonical sensory gain-control computation extended to value coding for choice

frameworks.name: value-based choice model
frameworks.one_line: choice model in which option values are normalized by context

frameworks.name: rational choice theory
frameworks.one_line: classical axioms violated by context-dependent normalized value signals

core_question: Does divisive normalization of value signals shape context-dependent choice behavior in primates and humans?
key_assumptions: value signals are encoded by a normalization computation analogous to sensory cortex
stochastic choice reads out normalized value representations
context is defined by the value and number of alternative options
manipulations: number of alternative options in the choice set
cross-species comparison between monkeys and humans
key_findings: choice models with normalization predict significant context-dependent choice phenomena
monkey choosers exhibit novel context-dependent behavior consistent with normalization
human choosers exhibit the same context-dependent behavior
predicted choice effects are classically irrational under standard axioms
interpretations: normalization of value signals critically shapes stochastic choice behavior
context effects in decision making can be unified within a quantitative normalization framework
